famous_genealogy_problems: Russian Empire surname adoption and patronymic instability; Jewish metrical books may mix Hebrew, Yiddish, Russian and civil-script entries; many fonds require locality-level identification; wartime access and repository disruption; guberniya/uyezd borders changed repeatedly; German-colony records may use Latin or German handwriting.
Indexes are finding aids, not proof; AutoTreeGen should require image/citation verification.
Historical boundaries and modern country borders often route the same record to different archives.
Endogamy, pedigree collapse and repeated surnames can inflate DNA-based relationship estimates.
Locality disambiguation is usually more important than surname search alone.
name_transliteration_notes: Normalize original-script name, transliteration, phonetic variant, patronymic/matronymic, maiden name and married name separately. Keep evidence-linked spelling per source. Use historical orthography and jurisdiction-aware transliteration; never overwrite original spelling.
common_fabrication_patterns_local: forged rabbinical descent through unsupported 18th-century ancestor
invented noble/Cossack line from surname resemblance
assuming all same-surname shtetl families are one family
using modern oblast for pre-1917 archive routing
fabricated Mennonite bridge ancestors based only on small segments

Historical boundaries and modern country borders often route the same record to different archives.

Indexes are finding aids, not proof; AutoTreeGen should require image/citation verification.
Historical boundaries and modern country borders often route the same record to different archives.
Endogamy, pedigree collapse and repeated surnames can inflate DNA-based relationship estimates.

assuming all imperial records are in modern Russian repositories

famous_genealogy_problems: Civil registration is mainly departmental/commune based, not centralized nationally; Paris 1871 fires destroyed many earlier civil/parish registers but reconstructions exist; Alsace-Lorraine switches language/jurisdiction; privacy limits recent BMD; cadastral and military records are often departmental.
Indexes are finding aids, not proof; AutoTreeGen should require image/citation verification.
Historical boundaries and modern country borders often route the same record to different archives.
Endogamy, pedigree collapse and repeated surnames can inflate DNA-based relationship estimates.
Locality disambiguation is usually more important than surname search alone.
name_transliteration_notes: Normalize original-script name, transliteration, phonetic variant, patronymic/matronymic, maiden name and married name separately. Keep evidence-linked spelling per source. Use historical orthography and jurisdiction-aware transliteration; never overwrite original spelling.
common_fabrication_patterns_local: invented noble line via same surname
ignoring Paris record loss/reconstituted records
wrong commune due to departmental homonyms
assuming Geneanet tree equals evidence

famous_genealogy_problems: England/Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland have different systems; census access is decennial and privacy-restricted for recent censuses; parish boundaries and registration districts changed; common names require certificate-level correlation; nonconformist and Jewish records may be outside Anglican parish series.
Indexes are finding aids, not proof; AutoTreeGen should require image/citation verification.
Historical boundaries and modern country borders often route the same record to different archives.
Endogamy, pedigree collapse and repeated surnames can inflate DNA-based relationship estimates.
Locality disambiguation is usually more important than surname search alone.

common_fabrication_patterns_local: wrong person selected from common-name census hit
assuming county boundaries stayed fixed
linking to aristocracy without probate/land evidence
ignoring mother's maiden name and informant evidence

famous_genealogy_problems: Major record losses occurred in the 1922 Public Record Office fire; 1901 and 1911 are the first complete surviving all-Ireland censuses online; civil registration begins 1864 for births/deaths and most marriages, 1845 for non-Catholic marriages; townland identification is critical; many parish registers are not indexed fully.
Indexes are finding aids, not proof; AutoTreeGen should require image/citation verification.
Historical boundaries and modern country borders often route the same record to different archives.
Endogamy, pedigree collapse and repeated surnames can inflate DNA-based relationship estimates.
Locality disambiguation is usually more important than surname search alone.

Keep evidence-linked spelling per source. Use historical orthography and jurisdiction-aware transliteration; never overwrite original spelling.
common_fabrication_patterns_local: claiming clan/chief descent from surname alone
wrong townland/county due to repeated placenames
using Griffith's Valuation as proof of parentage
ignoring parish/register gaps

Indexes are finding aids, not proof; AutoTreeGen should require image/citation verification.
Historical boundaries and modern country borders often route the same record to different archives.

famous_genealogy_problems: BDM is state-based and privacy-restricted for recent events; immigration, naturalisation, defence and internment records are often federal; newspapers are unusually important through Trove; convict records are colony-specific; Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander research needs culturally sensitive sources and access protocols.
Indexes are finding aids, not proof; AutoTreeGen should require image/citation verification.
Historical boundaries and modern country borders often route the same record to different archives.
Endogamy, pedigree collapse and repeated surnames can inflate DNA-based relationship estimates.
Locality disambiguation is usually more important than surname search alone.

Keep evidence-linked spelling per source. Use historical orthography and jurisdiction-aware transliteration; never overwrite original spelling.
common_fabrication_patterns_local: claiming convict descent from same-name person without locality proof
wrong state BDM index selected
assuming federal archive has all vital records
using newspaper mention as proof without civil/source correlation
